scenario: A team's customer support agents use Tasklist on their mobile devices (tablets, phones) when in the field. They want to ensure the experience is mobile-friendly.
question: Is Tasklist mobile-friendly out of the box?

options.text: Tasklist has no mobile browser support and only functions on desktop computers -- this misrepresents Tasklist's web-based delivery model. Tasklist is a browser-rendered Camunda web application that renders in any modern browser, including mobile browsers on tablets and phones. Camunda Forms rendered within Tasklist use the form-js library, which adapts to viewport dimensions. 8 documentation does not make a formal "responsive design" claim, the technology stack inherently supports mobile browser access. 8 product catalogue.

Tasklist is optimised exclusively for desktop browsers and does not work on mobile devices -- this overstates the desktop restriction. While desktop browsers provide the optimal Tasklist experience, the web-based Tasklist application functions on mobile browsers. Standard Camunda Forms render and can be completed on tablet and phone browsers. For field service or approval workflows on mobile, Tasklist's web UI provides functional access. A dedicated mobile-optimised native app is a separate consideration requiring custom development.

8, no native mobile application for Tasklist is documented or shipped as part of the product. The primary delivery is a web application accessed through mobile browsers. For deep mobile UX requirements including offline capability, push notifications, or native gestures, teams must build custom mobile applications using the Tasklist v2 REST API.

Tasklist's web UI is a browser-rendered Camunda webapp usable from any modern browser including mobile / tablet. 8 corpus. md:13-14 "User tasks can be automatically assigned to users and groups in the BPMN process, or they must be self-assigned from Tasklist. Once assigned to a user, the task can be completed. ") and Forms (the same Camunda Forms render the same way in any browser). Mobile-specific considerations therefore depend on the embedding client:

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist's web UI uses responsive design -- adapts to viewport. Mobile experience: Functional: task list, task details, completion all work on tablets and phones via web browsers. Forms render responsively: form-js library adapts. Standard web touch: tap, scroll, etc. Limitations: requires connectivity (no offline cache by default); web-app limitations on iOS / Android (no native push notifications, etc.). For richer mobile UX: Embed Tasklist API: build a custom mobile app using the Tasklist API; provides full control over UX (offline, push notifications, native gestures, etc.). Progressive Web App (PWA): configure as PWA for installable web app with limited offline. Native partner integrations: some Camunda partners offer mobile apps. For typical field service / approval use cases: responsive web Tasklist is usually sufficient. For specialised needs, custom app development is the path.
